title: ABOM Enforcement Policy v0.1
description: A declarative policy that ABOM enforces against an agent. The same policy document is consumed in two places: (1) the runtime gate / orchestrator, which decides ALLOW/DENY for a proposed action before it runs, and (2) `abom verify`, which audits a signed Composition Manifest and its Action Provenance chain after the fact. Every field below maps to a concrete check in the reference CLI (cli/src/abom/bom.py for verify; cli/src/abom/policy.py and gate.py for runtime). Inspired by CycloneDX and OPA/Rego concepts; not a CycloneDX artifact. Fields are deny-by-default where an allowlist is set: an absent allowlist means “unconstrained”, an empty allowlist means “deny all”.

policy.description: Policy schema version. Present on first-class policy documents.

name.description: Human-readable policy identifier, e.g. "finance-baseline" or "default". Surfaced in findings and decision cards.

description.description: Free-text summary of what this policy is for and which obligations it gestures at. Not enforced — documentation only.

allowed_models.description: Allowlist of model identifiers the agent may declare and call. Enforced by the `model_allowlist` rule at two layers: (a) every component of type "model" in the Composition Manifest must be on this list, and (b) every model named in a runtime `model_calls` entry must be on this list. A value NOT on the list yields a finding; a runtime model absent from the signed manifest additionally yields `composition_drift`. Omit the field entirely to leave models unconstrained. Identifiers should match exactly what `abom scan` records (e.g. "OpenAI (SDK)", "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022", "local/qwen2.5-coder").

allowed_egress_endpoints.description: Allowlist of network endpoints the agent's tools may reach. Enforced by the `egress_allowlist` rule: any `tools_invoked[].endpoint` in the provenance chain that is non-empty and not on this list yields a finding. Omit to leave egress endpoints unconstrained; set to [] to deny all egress endpoints. Match the endpoint strings exactly as recorded at runtime.

no_egress_classifications.description: Data classifications that must never leave the boundary. Enforced by the `residency` rule: any `data_touched` entry with `egress: true` AND a `classification` in this set yields a high-severity finding. Classifications are free-form strings agreed between the agent owner and policy author; common values are "confidential" and "restricted".

egress_allowed.description: Runtime gate toggle (cli/src/abom/policy.py): when false, the orchestrator treats outbound network egress as denied by default. Coarser than `allowed_egress_endpoints` (which is an audit-time allowlist); use `egress_allowed: false` for a hard deny-by-default at runtime and `allowed_egress_endpoints` to audit which specific endpoints were reached.

consequential_actions.description: Rules that mark a proposed action as “consequential” (i.e. requiring approval when `approval_required_for_consequential` is true). Two rule forms are recognized by the runtime: the literal token "writes_outside_workspace", and "touches_paths:<glob>" which matches changed file paths against an fnmatch glob.

approval_required_for_consequential.description: Runtime gate toggle: when true, any action classified consequential by `consequential_actions` requires human approval before it proceeds.

require_approval_when.description: Audit-time counterpart to `approval_required_for_consequential`, used by `abom verify`. When an action's `policy_decisions` include a result of "required", the provenance must carry an `approval` with `decision: "approved"`; otherwise the `approval_coverage` rule yields a finding. "consequential" is the reference default.

max_iterations_cap.description: Runtime safety cap on the agent's reasoning/tool-call loop. The orchestrator refuses to exceed this many iterations regardless of other policy.
